acceptance_summary: The tracker exists, docs/maintenance/README.md points to it, current source and dependency baselines are recorded, and the final goal contract rejects compatibility fallback backends.
verification_summary: Tracker files are present; docs/maintenance/README.md points to them; CSV parser check passed for 22 files; git diff --check passed.

checkpoint: Dependency policy and target library decisions
acceptance_summary: Target libraries, removed libraries, retained native domains, version sources, and packaging ownership are recorded before code migration begins.
verification_summary: CM-002 checkpoint rows are verified. dependency-ledger.csv records selected target libraries, selected removal candidates, conditional/deferred dependencies, and packaging ownership. capability-ledger.csv and overview.md agree on retained native ED2K and storage ownership. Current CMake, README, and packaging files still expose legacy dependencies; removal is explicitly deferred to implementation checkpoints CM-003 through CM-018.

acceptance_summary: The build detects target dependencies, rejects unsupported combinations clearly, records the C++ standard boundary, and removes obsolete dependency switches when safe.
verification_summary: C++17 is the project baseline. CMake now requires libcurl, Boost.JSON, OpenSSL for SSL, zlib when enabled, and libtorrent-rasterbar for BitTorrent. aria2_core links the target dependency set. Obsolete WinTLS, GnuTLS, nettle, GMP, libgcrypt, and GnuTLS policy build switches and generated-config defines are removed from the build baseline. Configure and build passed; stale build-option scans passed.

acceptance_summary: A Boost.Asio-owned runtime boundary exists for timers, wakeups, signal handling, shutdown, and library event integration without changing protocol behavior yet.
libcurl multi-socket integration can drive at least one ranged HTTP/HTTPS transfer through aria2-next request, storage, progress, and error boundaries.
HTTP/HTTPS redirects, headers, auth, proxying, resume, range requests, decoding, timeout, and completion use libcurl, and native HTTP client code is removed.

checkpoint: FTP FTPS SFTP and SCP migration
acceptance_summary: FTP, FTPS, SFTP, and SCP downloads use libcurl, and native FTP/SFTP/libssh2 command ownership is removed.

checkpoint: Cookie auth proxy and netrc cleanup
acceptance_summary: Cookie, auth, netrc, proxy, and browser-cookie behavior is either mapped to libcurl or pruned, and obsolete native parsers are removed.
JSON-RPC over HTTP uses Boost.Beast transport while existing supported RPC method semantics remain stable, and native HTTP server framing is removed.
WebSocket RPC and notifications use Boost.Beast, authorization boundaries remain correct, and wslay/native WebSocket code is removed.
Supported JSON parsing and serialization use Boost.JSON, and custom JSON parser/writer code is removed or reduced to a non-JSON variant boundary.
XML-RPC is removed, Metalink is either pruned or reduced to a pugixml-backed Metalink4 path, and obsolete XML parser ownership is removed.
OpenSSL owns TLS and supported crypto, stale GnuTLS/nettle/libgcrypt/GMP/WinTLS/internal fallback ownership is removed, and ED2K hash needs are explicitly retained or mapped.
Storage exposes verified progress, in-flight progress, flush, resume, hash, and control-file cleanup semantics that prevent 99 percent false completion.
Native ED2K protocol logic remains supported while shared runtime, crypto, compression, storage, and status integration move off obsolete infrastructure where practical.
BitTorrent remains libtorrent-only, and leftover native torrent/magnet/bencode helpers are removed unless they protect current ingress or metadata behavior.

checkpoint: Option docs and compatibility claim pruning
acceptance_summary: Help output, manual, completion, README, API option docs, and stale compatibility claims match the modern supported surface.
Release scripts, Dockerfiles, workflow files, dependency records, static closure checks, and runtime dependency scans match the modern dependency set.
Default configure, build, CTest, version, stale scans, packaging checks, and legal public smoke evidence pass; all tracker checkpoints are verified.
